fr.elements: Main Building; Roof System; Front Driveway; Landscaped Area; Detached Structure; Perimeter Fencing
fr.considerations: Dense Vegetation; Single Access Point; Limited Rear Access
fr.recommendations: Enhancing the clarity and availability of entry and exit routes by trimming or removing dense vegetation could facilitate swift and unobstructed responder access to the property. Establishing additional access points, especially at the rear of the property, may enhance the movement and flexibility of emergency operations, providing more efficient options for approach and departure. Clear visibility for the main building and other structures may be achieved by ensuring that landscaping around these areas is well-maintained and does not impede responder navigation. Moreover, designating specific areas for staging and ensuring these areas are free from obstructions could support organized and effective emergency response activities. By focusing on these recommendations, emergency responders may navigate and operate on the property with greater ease, efficiency, and safety.

wildfire.conditions: Scattered Trees; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Tree Cluster; Dense Tree Canopy; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Terrain; Vegetation Adjacent to Access Route; Vegetated Access Route
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space by increasing separation between the scattered trees and the structure, as well as managing the vegetation directly adjacent to the building, could help reduce exposure and improve property preparedness. Reducing fuel continuity within the tree cluster and beneath the dense tree canopy by thinning or pruning may limit potential fire spread. Establishing and maintaining an open defensible zone between the vegetated perimeter and the structure, while managing vegetation concentrations along the access route, may support improved access for firefighting efforts and reduce opportunities for fire to reach the property. In the open terrain surrounding the structure, preserving and enhancing existing fuel breaks could contribute to long-term wildfire resilience. Maintaining heightened awareness and regular management of vegetation near the access routes can support continuous suppression efforts and firefighter accessibility, ensuring better preparedness for a potential wildfire event.
